data.content_ref: inline:{"analogy":{"adopted_commitment_refs":[],"disanalogies":["Route binding is pre-execution policy, not runtime evidence","School-to-route binding is a configuration concern, not a reasoning artifact"],"overturn_conditions":["If schools are purely prompt conditioning with no route semantics, the analogy to policy freeze does not apply"],"shared_structure":["Immutable policy freeze binds runtime dispatch decisions","Append-only events record consequential transitions","Replay materializes canonical state from events"],"source_memory_refs":["731938a568fa4a6200024c0bcf743cf46f14b0ead5088c5e33ef1ac2c558d5b1","f1f1461eefbbba50ea2f9d6f87e9aed22230fa4538fe1c22fddbc15aaeec0b83"],"transfer_claims":["Just as the manifest freezes scratch/bridge policies, it should freeze school-to-route bindings to prevent prompt-delegated route selection"]},"claim":"The present failure is a manifest-policy gap: the immutable manifest freezes role routes and scratch/bridge policies but does not bind school conditioning to distinct configured routes. The scheduler iterates schools as prompt provenance but not as distinct wire-contract routes, creating an ambiguity where the harness attempts to express school-specific orchestration through model conditioning rather than deterministic route assignment. The harness should own school-to-route binding in the manifest; model calls should remain bounded content functions unaware of school routing. This preserves open-ended reasoning (C10) by ensuring the model receives only a single bounded responsibility (C4) while the harness deterministically selects which route, budget, and scratch/bridge policy applies.","counterconditions":[{"case":"If schools are documented as prompt-provenance-only conditioning with no route implication, the binding is unnecessary","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If the adapter already implicitly selects routes based on school context, the gap may not exist","eval":"observation"},{"case":"If future chat services require dynamic school assignment post-manifest-freeze, static binding would be too rigid","eval":"observation"}],"definitions":[],"derivation":[],"formalization_refs":[],"mechanism":"Deterministic components: manifest freeze binds school identifiers to route leases (extending run_manifest.py:412-500); scheduler resolves school to route lease before dispatch (extending scheduler.py:410-520); adapter verifies route lease against manifest binding (extending adapter.py:300-430); budget reservations enforced per-route against shared ceiling (budget.py:79-158). Model-call components: adapter performs schema-bound call with strict wire contract; model receives school as prompt/provenance conditioning only. Typed interfaces: SchoolRouteBinding{school_id, route_id, budget_tier, scratch_policy, bridge_policy} -> RouteLease -> WireContract. Transition semantics: manifest freeze is atomic; school-to-route binding is conflict-safe (run_manifest.py:1328-1406); route lease issuance is append-only event. Manifest/events/replay implications: replay materializes formal state only (harness.py:69-116); school route bindings are part of immutable manifest and replay deterministically. Evidence and security behaviour: adapter rejects mechanism drift (adapter.py:300-430); budget enforced before dispatch. Migration: extend manifest schema to include school-to-route binding map; scheduler reads binding before dispatch; no change to model adapter contract. Retained open-ended freedom: model still produces conjectures, criticisms, scratch content within its bounded responsibility. Strongest likely failure mode: binding all schools to the same route silently, making the binding a no-op. Falsifying tests: replay a run with two schools and assert each dispatched call's route lease matches the manifest binding; assert scheduler raises if no binding exists for a conditioned school; assert manifest digest changes when school-to-route binding changes.","premises":[],"schema":"deepreason-reasoning-envelope-v1","scope":{"covers":[],"excludes":[]},"simulation_refs":[],"uncertainties":[]}
